results.description: Bilayer head-to-head thickness D_HH (Tanford volume + Nagle A_L)
Area per lipid A_L (Tanford V_c / chain length from D_HH)
Bending modulus kappa (elastic estimate vs fluctuation spectroscopy)

details.note: Thin-plate elastic model over-predicts due to chain conformational entropy; experimental kappa=20 kBT from Evans & Rawicz (1990).

results.description: Melting temperature T_m (empirical chain-length scaling, Marsh 2013)
Partition capacity C(n) = 2n^2 (algebraic identity, n=1..8)
Critical packing parameter CPP in (0.5, 1.0) -> bilayer geometry
Sk in [0, 1] for arbitrary K-symbol probability distributions
Lipschitz bound |DSk| / ||Dp||_1 <= L_k  (K=8)

details.formula: L_k = (1 + ln K) / ln K  [Theorem 4, Paper 2]

results.description: Fisher metric g = diag(g_k, g_t, g_e) > 0 for all NIST families
Six NIST chemical families separable in S-entropy space
Spectral discrimination ratio R = d_max/d_min in [1.09, 4.38]
Timescale hierarchy tau_k <= tau_t <= tau_e (Protocol Uniqueness)
Parseval conservation on Koopman decomposition (synthetic orbit)
Born energy barrier for Na+ crossing bilayer >> kBT (partition opacity)
Saturation formula k_arr = 1-product(1-k_i) vs Monte Carlo
Saturation criterion: sum(k_i) -> inf => k_arr -> 1  (k_i=0.05, N=200)
Single-channel conductances vs patch-clamp (Neher & Sakmann 1976)
Spectral overlap kernel k(X;t) = exp(-||S(X)-t||^2/2*sigma^2)
Array completeness: minimum N_c for k_arr >= 0.99 at k_i=0.10
Landauer floor ΔE_min = k_BT ln2 at 310 K
ATP free energy |ΔG_ATP| ≈ 20 k_BT (physiological conditions)
dx/dATP = |ΔG_ATP| / (k_BT ln 3) ≈ 18.2 ternary steps per ATP
Electrochemical gradient ΔμNa per ion ≈ 5.11 k_BT (outward)
Electrochemical gradient ΔμK per ion ≈ 0.71 k_BT (inward, net)
Na⁺/K⁺-ATPase efficiency η = (3ΔμNa + 2ΔμK) / |ΔG_ATP| ≈ 84%

details.stoichiometry: 3 Na⁺ out, 2 K⁺ in, 1 ATP [Skou 1957; Albers 1967]

results.description: ATP diffusion time across 10-µm cell < 100 ms

details.criterion: τ_diff < 100 ms  (fast supply to pump sites)

results.description: Ternary optimality: argmin_{b>=2} {b/ln(b)} = 3 (closest integer to e)
Depth k = ceil(log3(N_cat)) for representative N_cat values
P_correct >= 0.95 for nc=44, kappa=0.10, k=3 (prototype validation)

details.note: k=3 prototype passes 0.95 threshold; k=12 requires nc>52

results.description: Energy per query E = k * kBT * ln3  at k=12
ATP equivalence: E_query / |DG_ATP| < 1 ATP per query (k=12)
Reynolds number Re = rho*v*w/mu ~= 0.014 << 1 (laminar)
Peclet number Pe = v*w/D ~= 10 (convection-dominated routing)

details.interpretation: Pe > 1 -> diffusion does not smear stream boundaries

results.description: Quantization error bound delta_Q = 3^{-k/3}/2  at k=12
Minimum depth k_min = 15 for dQ=0.003  (Quantization Fidelity Theorem)
Single-level parity error detection: 100% (exhaustive check)

details.proof: Single error changes group digit sum mod 3 by 1 or 2 (never 0), so parity sum != 0 always.  100% detection is analytic.

results.description: Multi-level parity detection rate > 85% (Monte Carlo, kappa_err=0.05)
Strobe Nyquist: tau_sense >= 2*tau_signal, hierarchy preserved
Simulated macro-F1 >= 0.90 on 6-class synthetic NIST data (k=12)
